vocabulary.name: Foundation NFT Marketplace Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: Domain vocabulary derived from the Foundation subgraph GraphQL schema and smart contract protocol for the Foundation NFT marketplace on Ethereum.

terms.definition: An Ethereum wallet address that has interacted with the Foundation marketplace. Tracks NFT ownership, auction activity, bids placed and accepted, transfers, FETH balances, and revenue earned.
An account that has minted at least one NFT on Foundation. Tracks total net sales and revenue in ETH from primary and secondary sales.
An ERC-721 smart contract that governs a collection of NFTs. Contains the token name, symbol, and base URI used to construct token metadata URIs.
A unique non-fungible token on the Foundation marketplace. Identified by contract address and token ID, linked to a creator, owner, auction history, offers, and buy-now listings.
A record of an ERC-721 token transfer on-chain, capturing sender, recipient, timestamp, and transaction hash.
The Foundation marketplace smart contract that manages auctions, offers, and buy-now listings.
A timed auction for an NFT on Foundation. Tracks seller, highest bidder, reserve price, start/end times, status, and revenue distribution among creator, owner, and protocol.
A bid placed in a Foundation auction. Tracks bidder, amount in ETH, timestamp, transaction hash, and auction reference.
An offer made to buy an NFT at a fixed price with an expiry. Can be accepted, canceled, outbid, expired, or invalidated.
A fixed-price listing for an NFT that allows immediate purchase. Can be accepted, canceled, or invalidated.
A contract that distributes ETH revenue among multiple recipients according to defined percentage shares. Used for collaborative NFT revenue splits.
Foundation ETH (FETH) escrow entry. Tracks escrowed ETH locked for bids and the expiry of that escrow.
An event log entry capturing actions taken on an NFT such as minting, listing, bidding, and transfers.
An ERC-721 approval granted by an NFT owner to a spender address.
Enumeration of auction lifecycle states: Open, Finalized, or Canceled.
Enumeration of offer lifecycle states: Open, Outbid, Expired, Canceled, Accepted, or Invalidated.
Enumeration of buy-now listing lifecycle states: Open, Canceled, Accepted, or Invalidated.
The total ETH earned by a creator or account from NFT sales, excluding any pending amounts.
The total ETH received from sales of NFTs minted by a creator.
Boolean flag indicating whether an NFT sale is the first sale on Foundation by the original creator.
The minimum ETH amount that must be bid to activate a Foundation auction.
An Ethereum address that referred a buyer to a Foundation listing and is entitled to a referral fee.
The IPFS content path appended to the contract baseURI to construct the full metadata URI for an NFT.
An optional address to receive NFT revenue and creator royalty payments, which may be a percent split contract.
A The Graph Protocol indexer deployment that listens to Ethereum events from Foundation smart contracts and stores the indexed data in a queryable GraphQL endpoint.
The production Ethereum blockchain network where Foundation smart contracts are deployed.
The Ethereum token standard for non-fungible tokens used by all NFTs on the Foundation marketplace.
